perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Affiliates" shall mean any entity controlling, controlled by or under the common control of the Purchaser. For the purpose of this Agreement, "control" shall mean the direct or indirect ownership of fifty (50%) percent or more of the outstanding shares or other voting rights of an entity or possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of management and policies of an entity.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Affiliates" shall mean any entity controlling, controlled by or under the common control of the Purchaser. For the purpose of this Agreement, "control" shall mean the direct ownership of twenty-five (25%) percent or more of the outstanding shares or other voting rights of an entity.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'control' is altered from 50% to 25%, creating a conflict with the standard legal understanding of control.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934 - Definition of Control
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text defines control as 25% ownership, which may not align with the legal definition used by courts when considering the Securities Exchange Act's implications regarding control persons and liability. The definition of control varies depending on context but generally requires a higher threshold than 25% for establishing control for liability purposes.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 230.405 - Definitions of terms. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.405 - Definitions of terms. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 230.405 Definitions of terms. Unless the context otherwise requires, all terms used in §§ 230.400 to 230.494, inclusive, or in the forms for registration have the same meanings as in the Act and in the general rules and regulations . In addition, the following definitions apply, unless the context otherwise requires: Affiliate. An affiliate of, or person affiliated with, a specified person, is a person that directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the person specified. Amount. The term amount, when used in regard to securities, means the principal amount if relating to evidences of indebtedness, the number of shares if relating to shares , and the number of units if relating to any other kind of security . Associate. The term associate, when used to indicate a relationship with any person, means (1) a corporation or organization (other than the registrant or a majority-owned subsidiary of the registrant) of which such person is an officer or partner or is, directly or indirectly, the beneficial owner of 10 percent or more of any class of equity securities, (2) any trust or other estate in which such person has a substantial beneficial interest or as to which such person serves as trustee or in a similar capacity, and (3) any relative or spouse of such person, or any relative of such spouse, who has the same home as such person or who is a director or officer of the registrant or any of its parents or subsidiaries. Automatic shelf registration statement. The term automatic shelf registration statement means a registration statement filed on Form S-3, Form F-3, or Form N-2 ( § 239.13 , § 239.33 , or §§ 239.14 and 274.11a-1 of this chapter) by

perturbation.justification: The modified text redefines 'control' as 25% ownership, whereas the standard legal understanding often requires a higher threshold. The law explanation states that the definition of control generally requires a higher threshold than 25% for establishing control for liability purposes. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'affiliate' as a person who 'directly, or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controls or is controlled by, or is under common control with, the person specified.' By lowering the threshold for control to 25%, the changed text broadens the scope of who is considered an 'affiliate,' which can have significant legal implications, particularly under the Securities Exchange Act as mentioned in the Law Explanation, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information or compilation of information not generally known to the public or the industry, that is proprietary or confidential to the Purchaser, its 2 <PAGE> Affiliates and/or those doing business with the Purchaser and/or its Affiliates, including but not limited to know-how, process, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Ermilio in the course of his past, present and future dealings with the Purchaser and its Affiliates, which is not readily available to the public.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the term "Confidential Information" means any information, regardless of its accessibility to the public, that the Purchaser deems confidential. This includes, but is not limited to, know-how, processes, techniques, methods, plans, specifications, trade secrets, patents, copyrights, supplier lists, customer lists, mailing lists, financial information, business plans and/or policies, methods of operation, sales and marketing plans and any other information acquired or developed by Ermilio in the course of his past, present and future dealings with the Purchaser and its Affiliates.
perturbation.explanation: The modified definition broadens 'Confidential Information' to include anything the Purchaser deems confidential, irrespective of public availability, which contradicts the trade secret definition requiring secrecy.
perturbation.law_explanation: The UTSA, widely adopted in the US, defines trade secrets as information that derives independent economic value from not being generally known or readily ascertainable. By including publicly available information as 'Confidential Information,' the modified contract contradicts this fundamental element of trade secret law, potentially rendering the clause unenforceable regarding publicly available information.

perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines 'Confidential Information' to include anything the Purchaser deems confidential, regardless of its public availability. This contradicts the fundamental requirement of trade secret law, which necessitates that the information derives economic value from not being generally known or readily ascertainable, as pointed out in the Law Explanation. Although no actual law snippet was provided, the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation highlight that making publicly available information 'Confidential Information' directly undermines the established legal understanding and protection afforded to trade secrets, thus creating a legal contradiction under Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that the restrictive covenants contained in Section 1 and/or Section 2 of this Agreement shall be found by a court of competent jurisdiction to be unreasonable by reason of such restrictive covenants extending for too great a period of time or over too great a geographic area or by reason of such restrictive covenants being too extensive in any other respect, then such restrictive covenant shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make such restrictive covenant reasonable and enforceable under the circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that the restrictive covenants contained in Section 1 and/or Section 2 of this Agreement shall be found by any arbitrator to be unreasonable, then such restrictive covenant shall be deemed automatically waived and void, with no further modification possible.
perturbation.explanation: Replaced 'court of competent jurisdiction' with 'any arbitrator', and changed the 'blue pencil' clause to automatic waiver, conflicting with court jurisdiction on contract enforceability.
perturbation.law_explanation: While the FAA generally supports arbitration, Section 10 allows courts to vacate arbitration awards under specific circumstances, including where the arbitrator exceeded their powers. By stating that any unreasonable covenant found by an arbitrator is automatically waived and void, without possibility for judicial review or modification, the contract limits the court's power to review the arbitration, potentially conflicting with the FAA.

perturbation.justification: The modification contradicts the FAA by removing the court's ability to review an arbitrator's decision, as the changed text states that an unreasonable covenant is automatically waived and void. The original text allowed for modification to the minimum extent necessary. The law snippet (9 USC 10) outlines grounds for a United States court to vacate an arbitration award, implying judicial oversight that the modified contract attempts to eliminate. The modified text conflicts with the court's power to review arbitration outcomes.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New York applicable to agreements made and to be performed in that state, without regard to any of its principles of conflicts of laws or other laws that would result in the application of the laws of another jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of New Jersey, irrespective of where the agreement is performed or the parties reside.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to New Jersey while the company is in Florida and the contract mentions New York creates a conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Full Faith and Credit Clause, U.S. Constitution
perturbation.law_explanation: The Full Faith and Credit Clause requires states to respect the laws and judicial decisions of other states. While parties can generally choose a governing law, courts may refuse to enforce that choice if it violates public policy or bears no reasonable relationship to the transaction. If the contract's performance or parties' location has a strong nexus to a state other than New Jersey, a court might disregard the New Jersey choice-of-law provision, raising a conflict with the intended application of the clause.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Article IV | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Article IV Primary tabs Section 1. Full faith and credit shall be given in each state to the public acts, records, and judicial proceedings of every other state. And the Congress may by general laws prescribe the manner in which such acts, records, and proceedings shall be proved, and the effect thereof. Section 2. The citizens of each state shall be entitled to all privileges and immunities of citizens in the several states. A person charged in any state with treason, felony, or other crime, who shall flee from justice, and be found in another state, shall on demand of the executive authority of the state from which he fled, be delivered up, to be removed to the state having jurisdiction of the crime. No person held to service or labor in one state, under the laws thereof, escaping into another, shall, in consequence of any law or regulation therein, be discharged from such service or labor, but shall be delivered up on claim of the party to whom such service or labor may be due. Section 3. New states may be admitted by the Congress into this union; but no new states shall be formed or erected within the jurisdiction of any other state; nor any state be formed by the junction of two or more states, or parts of states, without the consent of the legislatures of the states concerned as well as of the Congress. The Congress shall have power to dispose of and make all needful rules and regulations respecting the territory or other property belonging to the United States; and nothing in this Constitution shall be so construed as to prejudice any claims of the United States, or of any particular state. Section 4. The United States shall guarantee to every state in this union a republican form of government, and shall protect each of them against invasion; and on application of the legislature, or of the executive (when the legislature cannot be convened) against domestic violence. ‹ Article III up Article V › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Ser
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies New York law, while the changed text specifies New Jersey law. The Perturbation Explanation states the company is in Florida and the contract mentions New York, creating a conflict when changing the governing law to New Jersey. The Law Explanation suggests that courts may disregard the chosen law if it violates public policy or lacks a reasonable relationship to the transaction. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing the Full Faith and Credit Clause, indicates that states must respect the laws of other states. Therefore, switching the governing law to New Jersey, when the contract and company have ties to New York and Florida, can be seen as an attempt to circumvent the established legal framework, leading to a potential contradiction.
